label: frameup

name: reporting
function: ReportWriter: one dated-report seam under reports/<category>/ for every producer.
label: reporting

name: functions
function: Functions check: run each cataloged part's live demo, or cite its test twin.
label: functions

name: terminal
function: In-game computer terminal: wires the diagnostic renderers behind one console.
label: terminal

name: dependencies
function: the dependency gate: every declared dependency must be justified in the ledger
label: dependencies
notes: Reads pyproject.toml + dependency_ledger.toml (stdlib tomllib); frameless.

name: bench
function: engine-tick performance evidence: handle_command throughput + latency
label: bench
notes: Frameless (stdlib time+statistics); files dated evidence under reports/performance/.

name: dashboard
function: the readiness Lens: a server-rendered HTML board + JSON twin over real forge evidence
label: dashboard
notes: Frameless full-stack proof (stdlib html.escape, no template engine); mounted on parts/api.py at GET / and GET /api/status.

name: blueprint
function: the Blueprint: a software idea forged into a validated JSON+Markdown spec
label: blueprint
notes: Frameless (stdlib json+re); fail-loud validator; state is JSON, Markdown/HTML are projections.

name: blueprint_render
function: project a Blueprint to a static, accessible HTML/CSS page (the renderer spine)
label: blueprint_render
notes: Frameless (stdlib html.escape, no template engine); rendered pages are regenerable evidence under reports/blueprints/.

name: config
function: one typed, validated view of the environment (pydantic Settings), credentials redacted
label: config
notes: Pydantic model catalogs the env (PORT, DATABASE_URL, CODEFORGE_ARCHITECT, ...); fails loud on a bad value; the `config` terminal program renders it.

name: blueprint_ai
function: draft a Blueprint from a freeform idea with Claude (schema-enforced structured output)
label: blueprint_ai
notes: Anthropic messages.parse (output_format schema); injected client (mockable, CI-safe); AI output is re-validated by from_dict and always a Tier-4 draft. One API key away.

name: observability
function: structured request logs (structlog) + a Prometheus /metrics endpoint on the HTTP surface
label: observability
notes: One HTTP middleware times each request, emits a JSON log event, and records a metric by route TEMPLATE (bounded cardinality). Prometheus text exposition rendered by a stdlib registry (no scraping library).

name: foundry
function: propose/approve/generate pipeline (proving ground phases 9-10): human-approved, sandboxed, no-overwrite code generation
label: foundry
notes: A PatchProposal writes nothing; a human must approve; generation lands only in the git-ignored workspace/ sandbox, never overwrites, never escapes, never touches source/config/git. Owner-gated @forge verb.

name: character_view
function: build a score-sheet view model from a live job definition
label: character_view

function: derived combat statistics from the six attributes
label: derived

label: engineer

label: job_progress

name: score_sheet

label: score_sheet

label: loader_cache

label: stewardship_change

name: workflow
function: a config-driven workflow engine on the pure FSM: roles and history
label: workflow

notes: The first reusable vertical slice (docs/vision_resync.md).

name: onboarding
function: the practical adapter for the Workflow Engine: an onboarding checklist
label: onboarding
reuse: the practical adapter proving the workflow part reuses outside the game

name: quest
function: the game adapter for the Workflow Engine: a regional quest as a workflow
label: quest

name: manifest
function: typed Part Manifest: a reusable parts machine-readable contract
label: manifest
reuse: formal contract for any reusable part in the Hardware Store

name: assembly
function: discover what parts compose a product and file the evidence
label: assembly
reuse: import discovery and composition evidence for any assembled product

name: loop
function: trace a part through every manufacturing stage and file evidence
label: loop
reuse: end-to-end manufacturing loop trace for any part

name: token_bucket
function: rate limiting by the token-bucket algorithm: a sustained rate with bursts
label: token_bucket
reuse: rate-limiting core; one bucket, many adapters (game shout + practical login guard)
notes: Hardware Store part token-bucket; docs/hardware/token-bucket.yaml.

name: login_guard
function: the practical adapter for the token bucket: rate-limit login attempts
label: login_guard
reuse: brute-force login-attempt limiting; the practical face of the token bucket

name: chat_throttle
function: the game adapter for the token bucket: anti-spam on a player's shout
label: chat_throttle
notes: The `shout` verb, rate-limited by the token bucket.

name: retry
function: retry with exponential backoff: transient failures recover, permanent ones don't
label: retry
reuse: resilience core; one policy, many adapters (game calibrate + practical resilient_call)
notes: Hardware Store part retry-policy; docs/hardware/retry-policy.yaml.

name: resilient_call
function: the practical adapter for retry: an unreliable call with an audit trail
label: resilient_call
reuse: resilient API/DB calls with a recorded attempt history; the practical face of retry

name: calibrate
function: the game adapter for retry: auto-retry a flaky calibration
label: calibrate
notes: The `calibrate` verb, auto-retried by the retry core.

name: circuit_breaker
function: fail fast when a service is broken; test recovery after a timeout
label: circuit_breaker
reuse: resilience core built on the state-machine part; relay (game) + service_breaker (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part circuit-breaker; composes parts/statemachine; docs/hardware/circuit-breaker.yaml.

name: service_breaker
function: the practical adapter for the circuit breaker: per-service breakers
label: service_breaker
reuse: per-service circuit breakers so upstreams fail independently; the practical face

name: relay
function: the game adapter for the circuit breaker: a flaky power relay that can trip
label: relay
notes: The `channel` verb, protected by the circuit breaker.

name: repository
function: the Repository pattern: a collection-like boundary over stored entities
label: repository
reuse: persistence core: a typed Protocol + in-memory impl; logbook (game) + asset_registry (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part repository; docs/hardware/repository.yaml.

name: asset_registry
function: the practical adapter for the repository: a records/asset registry
label: asset_registry
reuse: storage-agnostic records/asset registry; the practical face of the repository

name: logbook
function: the game adapter for the repository: a player's numbered logbook
label: logbook
notes: The `journal` verb, a repository-backed logbook.

name: health
function: a health-check registry: run named checks, aggregate an honest overall status
label: health
reuse: observability core: health checks + honest aggregation; vitals (game) + service_health (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part health-registry; docs/hardware/health-registry.yaml.

name: service_health
function: the practical adapter for the health registry: a service readiness probe
label: service_health
reuse: service readiness/liveness from aggregated dependency checks; the practical face

name: vitals
function: the game adapter for the health registry: a world-vitals panel
label: vitals
notes: The `vitals` verb, a world-health panel.

name: validation
function: collect validation issues into a result: valid, or a list of clear errors
label: validation
reuse: validation core: composable rules + accumulated result; name_check (game) + payload_check (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part validator; docs/hardware/validator.yaml.

name: payload_check
function: the practical adapter for validation: validate a signup payload
label: payload_check
reuse: signup/form/API payload validation; the practical face of the validator

name: name_check
function: the game adapter for validation: preview whether a character name is valid
label: name_check
notes: The `namecheck` verb, a name-validity preview.

name: feature_flags
function: toggle features at runtime without a redeploy: typed flags with precedence
label: feature_flags
reuse: config core: runtime flags; features (game) + feature_control (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part feature-flags; docs/hardware/feature-flags.yaml.

name: feature_control
function: the practical adapter for feature flags: an environment kill switch
label: feature_control
reuse: env-driven kill switch / canary; the practical face of feature flags

name: features
function: the game adapter for feature flags: an in-world feature panel
label: features
notes: The `features` verb, a world feature-flag panel.

name: test_evidence
function: record check evidence honestly: missing evidence is never a pass
label: test_evidence
reuse: evidence core: honest quality gate; world_cert (game) + release_gate (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part test-evidence; docs/hardware/test-evidence.yaml.

name: release_gate
function: the practical adapter for test evidence: a release-readiness gate
label: release_gate
reuse: CI release-readiness gate; the practical face of test evidence

name: world_cert
function: the game adapter for test evidence: a world-readiness certificate
label: world_cert
notes: The `certify` verb, a world-readiness certificate.

name: plugin_registry
function: extend behavior by explicit registration: no dynamic code loading
label: plugin_registry
reuse: plugin core: explicit-registration registry; heralds (game) + exporters (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part plugin-registry; docs/hardware/plugin-registry.yaml.

name: exporters
function: the practical adapter for the plugin registry: pluggable export providers
label: exporters
reuse: pluggable export providers (json/csv); the practical face of the plugin registry

name: heralds
function: the game adapter for the plugin registry: pluggable in-world heralds
label: heralds
notes: The `heralds` verb, pluggable proclamations.

name: sanitizer
function: normalize untrusted text: strip control chars, collapse whitespace, cap length
label: sanitizer
reuse: input-normalization core; titles (game) + field_sanitizer (practical)
notes: Hardware Store part sanitizer; docs/hardware/sanitizer.yaml.

name: field_sanitizer
function: the practical adapter for the sanitizer: clean fields before storage/logs
label: field_sanitizer
reuse: clean stored/logged fields; the practical face of the sanitizer

name: titles
function: the game adapter for the sanitizer: set a clean player title
label: titles
notes: The `title` verb, a sanitized player title.

name: change_ledger
function: a Change/Patch engine: record changes and drive them through a gated lifecycle
label: change_ledger
reuse: change management: a role-gated, evidence-gated change/patch lifecycle, one core with many adapters
notes: Assembled from five Hardware Store parts; no code copied.

name: patch_tracker
function: the practical adapter for the change ledger: a software patch tracker
label: patch_tracker
reuse: track dependency bumps and CVE fixes through the gated lifecycle
notes: The practical life of the change ledger core.

name: maintenance
function: the game adapter for the change ledger: a world-maintenance log
label: maintenance
notes: The `maintenance` verb, the world's own gated change log.

name: arc
function: ARC (Assurance, Readiness, Control): compose gate verdicts into one honest report
label: arc
reuse: release-readiness dashboard: compose many gate verdicts into one honest verdict
notes: Composes eleven dimensions: six read the repo, release/evidence read filed verdicts (release via arc_ledger, evidence via the Chronicle), change/patch stay MISSING until a store is added, and Control reads the Chronicle (incidents + ai-eval regressions) to hold readiness.

name: stream_framer
function: frame a byte stream into complete messages, buffering partials
label: stream_framer
reuse: framing: any socket/pipe/protocol reader that must not split a message across reads
notes: Harvested from codeforge-client's line framer; addresses the endswith(b'') scar.

name: record_stream
function: the practical adapter for the stream framer: read records from a byte stream
label: record_stream
reuse: log tailers, protocol clients, delimited-record readers
notes: Practical life of the stream framer core.

name: telegraph
function: the game adapter for the stream framer: a dispatch that arrives in pieces
label: telegraph
notes: The `telegraph` verb, a bursty dispatch reframed into clean lines.

name: signal_bus
function: a typed signal bus: subscribe handlers by signal type, publish to them
label: signal_bus
reuse: eventing: decoupled typed pub/sub, one publisher many independent subscribers
notes: Harvested from codeforge-client's event bus; distinct from parts/events echo broadcasts.

name: notifier
function: the practical adapter for the signal bus: fan a domain event to handlers
label: notifier
reuse: webhook fan-out, audit trails, decoupled domain-event notification
notes: Practical life of the signal bus core.

name: chime
function: the game adapter for the signal bus: a gate-chime answers a world signal
label: chime
notes: The `chime` verb, a subscriber ringing on a published world signal.

name: harvest_lens
function: scan source for reusable-pattern candidates not yet in the Hardware Store
label: harvest_lens
reuse: automate Hardware Store gap analysis: detect harvestable patterns as code is written
notes: The `harvest` verb; V3 learning tool. Reads only, drafts candidates, never files.

name: store_index
function: organize the Hardware Store catalog by V3 engineering domain and address
label: store_index
reuse: catalog addressing + multi-field search: organize a parts catalog by engineering domain
notes: The `store` verb; V3 catalog realignment. Derives addresses, edits no card.

name: learning_record
function: capture an engineering improvement as evidence: what changed and why
label: learning_record
reuse: preserve institutional knowledge: capture why an improvement was made and what to learn
notes: The `learnings` verb; the LEARN step of the self-improving loop. Data-driven, fail-loud.

name: complexity
label: complexity
function: measure cyclomatic complexity per function (the Analyze station's third fold)
reuse: holistic code evaluation: flag high-complexity functions beyond tests/lint/coverage
notes: The `complexity` verb; stdlib ast, no dependency. Reads only.

name: clone_scan
label: clone_scan
function: find structurally duplicated logic across the codebase (AST shape hashing)
reuse: duplicate-logic detection: upgrade pattern discovery from signals to real clones
notes: The `clones` verb; upgrades the Harvest Lens with structural AST clone detection.

name: record_loader
function: load validated JSON records from a file or a directory
label: record_loader
reuse: a validated JSON-record loader parameterized by schema + error type
notes: Extracted from the blueprint/learning_record clone the scan found. Recommend cataloging (a promotion, Josh's call).

name: paths
function: resolve a path from an environment override or a default, in one place
label: paths
reuse: env-override path resolution: one helper for $VAR-or-default path config
notes: Extracted from the _default_path clone the scan found across 4 parts.

name: arc_ledger
function: file and read the runtime dimensions' verdicts as dated evidence for ARC
label: arc_ledger
reuse: dated verdict evidence: a gate files its verdict, a reader takes the latest, absence is honestly missing
notes: ARC slice 'Complete the Ten': release/evidence file real verdicts; change/patch await a persistent store.

name: chronicle
function: the Chronicle: an append-only, hash-chained record store the ship reads back (its memory)
label: chronicle
reuse: tamper-evident append-only memory: retained, content-hashed, cited records read back for trend/provenance
notes: Chronicle slice 1a: append-only hashed core + the evidence kind + a read-only chronicle verb; arc_ledger.emit retains its evidence verdict here. ARC reads evidence from the Chronicle in slice 1b.

name: ai_eval
function: score an Advisor's answer against a rubric and file it as a Chronicle ai-eval (eval-regression memory)
label: ai_eval
reuse: MLOps eval-regression memory: score an AI/Advisor output against a rubric, file it, track it over time behind the network seam
notes: Chronicle slice 5 (ai-eval). Advisor seam (LocalArchitect offline / ClaudeAdvisor live); keyword-coverage rubric, honest not semantic. `make ai-eval` runs the offline sample.

name: retention
function: hold-aware retention analysis for the Chronicle (read-only, R1): disposition not deletion
label: retention
reuse: records retention governance: an append-only store gets hold-aware, dry-run disposition analysis that never deletes
notes: Chronicle retention R1: read-only visibility + dry-run only; real disposition (R2) is owner-gated; physical destruction deferred. Federal rule #10 (any hold wins).

name: coupling
function: read-only engine coupling report: trace the runtime module closure per surface and classify what a cast could shed (detachment D1)
label: coupling
reuse: dependency/coupling analysis: trace a program's real runtime module closure to see what a slimmer package could shed
notes: Detachment D1 (read-only, keel-signed). Traces via subprocess import corpus; honest under-count caveat; D2 selective vendoring + D3 decoupling each need own keel sign-off.

name: hashchain
function: a general tamper-evident append-only hash-chained ledger (the Chronicle's reusable core)
label: hashchain
reuse: harvested Hardware Store part: append-only integrity ledger for audit trails, transaction logs, chain-of-custody
notes: The general primitive harvested from chronicle; chronicle now shares its canonical content_hash. Catalogued as hash-chained-ledger.

name: gmcp
function: structured out-of-band state frames for GMCP-aware clients
label: gmcp
reuse: GMCP framing + negotiation reading: any Telnet service that wants to push structured state to a capable client alongside the text stream
notes: Pure card; the TCP gateway owns the socket and decides when to send. Vitals derive through sheet_from_session (derive-don't-store). Consumes parts.telnet_codec for the IAC/SB/SE bytes and escape/negotiation codec.

name: telnet_codec
function: the Telnet byte codec: command bytes, IAC escaping, negotiation reading
label: telnet_codec
reuse: one home for the Telnet wire (RFC 854/855): any Telnet gateway or client that must strip inbound IAC, escape a subnegotiation body, or read an option reply without desyncing
notes: Extracted (batch 1 of the modularity audit) from gateway._strip_telnet (decode) + gmcp.escape_iac/enables_gmcp (encode), which had each re-declared IAC/SB/SE. Pure, socket-free; gateway consumes strip_iac, gmcp consumes escape_iac + read_negotiation.

name: score_sheet_model
function: the character score-sheet view model (plain, validated data)
label: score_sheet_model
notes: Split from score_sheet (the renderer) so the view model is consumed independently by character_view + equipment; makes the catalog's 'decoupled view model' claim literal.

name: progression_awards
function: grant XP/JP/TP and climb the curves (the leveling engine)
label: progression_awards
reuse: one home for the leveling engine: any reward source (a felled NPC, a finished quest, a future achievement) grants progress through the same three monotonic awards, without pulling in strike math
notes: Extracted (modularity audit) from combat.award_xp/award_jp/award_tp + _ascend_resources, so the damage loop and the leveling engine are separate responsibilities. Combat now imports the grants; quest's award_xp hook points here too.
